gaps: fedcourts query returned 0 rows for every --topic tried (from very specific down to 'criminal') on scotus; the topic index seems too sparse to surface cert priors, so no corpus priors informed this cell
a statpack cut for 'related petition granted / hold candidate' status would capture the single strongest signal in this cell better than relist count
the relist_bucket and cvsg statpack cuts are entirely (unknown), so the signal cuts the prompt points at were unusable
helpful: provisioned full petition and BIO text (untruncated) — the BIO's mootness and antecedent-grounds arguments were essential
snapshot's full ProceedingsandOrder list — the ten distributions and their stop date were the key docket signal
web search — found the companion Beaird grant that resolves the relist-then-silence pattern
notes: The decisive fact (Beaird v. United States, No. 25-5343, granted 2026-04-20 on the identical QP, with this petition evidently held for it) came from web search, not the corpus or CourtListener.
